fix(pipeline): use calendar-year season for Brasileirao in API-Football

Brasileirao runs Mar-Dec (calendar year), not Aug-May like European
leagues. The get_current_season() was returning 2025 in March 2026,
causing API-Football to return no fixtures/stats for Brasileirao.

Added calendar_year_season flag to LEAGUE_CONFIGS and APIFootballClient
so Brasileirao correctly uses season=2026.

Fix migration version conflict: renumber harmony_conditional to 0011

Two migrations shared version 0010 (draw_node and harmony_conditional),
causing VersionMismatch(10) on startup and crash-looping the app.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: handle out-of-range RamRa addresses in one-hot sparse commit

Non-memory cycles produce addr=k_chunk (out of range) from
buildRamRaPolynomial. The previous dense code silently skipped these
via `if (addr < k_chunk)`, but the one-hot PR replaced the guard with
`assert(addr < k_chunk)` causing crashes on all programs with RAM access.

commit.message:
fix: resolve compatibility issues for Litmus Edge 4.x

Dockerfile
- Keep original wheel filename so pip can validate the wheel metadata.
  Renaming to litmussdk.whl stripped the name-version-abi-platform format
  that pip requires.

base.html
- Replace Jinja2 block tags inside the HTML comment with plain-text bracket
  notation. Jinja2 processes {% %} tags even inside <!-- --> comments, so
  {% extends "base.html" %} caused infinite self-extension (RecursionError)
  and {% block content %} left an unclosed block (TemplateSyntaxError).

engine.py
- Download the DeviceHub driver record cache at startup via create_dh_cache().
  The SDK raises MissingRecordVersion if the cache is absent when a device is
  created. get_version() is used to match the cache version to the connected
  Litmus Edge instance. The call is a no-op if the version is already cached.

litmus_utils.py
- Add get_driver_id_by_name(): looks up a driver UUID by display name from the
  cached DriverRecord. The SDK's Device model requires a UUID — passing a name
  string causes a KeyError because _get_driver_from_value() uppercases the
  input and uses it as a UUID dict key.
- Add safe_delete_device_by_name(): deletes any pre-existing device with a
  given name before setup() creates a new one. Prevents CreateDevice failures
  when a previous partial run left an orphaned device (e.g. after a container
  restart mid-scenario that lost in-memory state).

dh_01 / dh_02 / dh_03 scenarios
- Pass Generator driver UUID via get_driver_id_by_name() instead of the
  string "generator".
- Remove non-existent updateRate device property (Generator on LE 4.x only
  accepts name and description, both already set as Device model fields).
- Change tag register from name="int" to name="S" (sinusoidal register).
- Change tag value_type from "int" to "int64" (supported type for register S).
- Remove "valueType" from tag properties dict — it is already serialised as
  the top-level ValueType GQL field by the Tag model's value_type field.
  Duplicating it causes a server-side validation failure (skipped N regs).
- Add required register properties: address, count, pollingInterval.
- Call safe_delete_device_by_name() at the start of each setup().

.gitignore
- Ignore .claude/ (Claude Code session data) and test_tags.py (debug script).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: litmus-edge-training-lab
repository.description: Hands-on troubleshooting trainer for Litmus Edge 4.x — injects real problems into a live platform instance for learners to diagnose and fix.

commit.message:
fix: use ChatOpenAI for LiteLLM proxy to avoid litellm_pre_call_utils 500 error

langchain_anthropic uses the /v1/messages Anthropic endpoint which triggers
litellm-proxy-extras pre-call processing and causes:
  ImportError: No module named 'litellm.types.proxy.litellm_pre_call_utils'

Fix: switch to ChatOpenAI pointing to /v1/chat/completions (OpenAI-compatible
endpoint) which bypasses that code path entirely. Also suppress the
create_react_agent deprecation warning since the suggested alternative import
does not exist in langchain 1.2.x.

Also install langchain-openai in the venv.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat(security): add approval context binding — approved object == executed object

Addresses the core security concern: "payments inside browser agents only
become safe when the approval context is stronger than the page context."

Changes:

1. ApprovalContext model (new) — first-class binding between approval and
   execution with origin, session, merchant, cart, action, policy, and
   payment fields. SHA-256 approval_context_hash verified at execution time.

2. ExecutionIntent — add origin_url, action_description, action_description_hash,
   session_context_hash, approved_at, approval_context_hash fields.

3. AttestationEnvelope — add origin_hash, action_description_hash,
   approval_timestamp to signed payload. Tampering any field after signing
   invalidates the Ed25519 signature.

4. MandateVerifier — verify action_description_hash matches SHA-256 of
   action_description during chain verification.

5. AP2 mandates — add natural_language_description, merchant_constraints,
   cart_hash, cart_confirmation_hash, payment_details_id, cart_mandate_hash,
   user_authorization_hash, approval_context_hash fields toward upstream
   Google AP2 conformance.

6. EIP-712 — new SardisPaymentApproval type signing sessionId, walletAddress,
   chainId, nonce, merchantDomain, amount, token, destination, cartHash,
   originHash (stronger than connect-only signing).

7. Attestation lookup — now payment-specific (joins on mandate_id/payment_id)
   instead of latest-for-agent.

8. audit_hash — checkout now includes merchant identity and cart context.

38 new tests covering all 8 mutation/replay scenarios from the security verdict.
1533 total tests pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: sardis
repository.description: Payment OS for the Agent Economy — single execution gateway with signed attestation envelopes, natural language policies, and non-custodial MPC wallets

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Phase 4 Session 1: E2EE backend + migration

Migration 012_e2ee_messages.sql:
  - ADD COLUMN messages.is_encrypted BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE
  - ADD COLUMN messages.recipient_id UUID (per-recipient group encryption)
  - CREATE INDEX idx_messages_recipient

Message model (app/models/message.py):
  - Add is_encrypted and recipient_id fields

encryption.py (app/services/encryption.py):
  - Document as legacy/transitional — Fernet kept for is_encrypted=FALSE
    messages so existing conversations remain readable
  - Pass-through comments clarify Phase 5 removal plan

messages.py (app/routers/messages.py):
  - format_message(): relay ciphertext as-is for is_encrypted=TRUE;
    Fernet-decrypt only for is_encrypted=FALSE (backward compat)
  - SendMessageRequest / CreateDirectRequest / CreateGroupRequest:
    add is_encrypted flag (default False)
  - GET /messages/{conv_id}/messages: filter by recipient_id for E2EE
  - POST /messages/{conv_id}/encrypted: new endpoint for group E2EE fan-out
  - GET /messages/{conv_id}/sync: lightweight sync — IDs + timestamps only
  - _push_message / _build_ws_payload: relay ciphertext for E2EE messages;
    decrypt for legacy so non-updated clients still work in real-time
  - Conversation last_message preview: "🔒 Encrypted message" for E2EE

users.py (app/routers/users.py):
  - GET /users/{user_id}/public-key: return X25519 public key (no auth)
  - PATCH /users/{user_id}/public-key: upload/rotate own public key
    (validated: must be exactly 32 bytes base64-encoded)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat(admin): add missing sprint default settings for duration and carry-over (PUNT-327) (#403)

## Summary

- Add `defaultSprintDuration` (Int, days 1-90) and
`defaultAutoCarryOver` (Boolean) fields to the `SystemSettings` Prisma
model
- Update the admin sprint settings form to include duration input and
auto-carryover toggle, matching the project-level sprint settings UI
- Wire new fields through the API route (validation + persistence),
system-settings utility, React Query hooks, and demo mode defaults
- Update project-level "Reset to System Defaults" to pull duration and
carry-over from system settings instead of hardcoded values
- Include new fields in database export schema and import logic for
backup compatibility

## Test plan

- [x] Navigate to Admin > Project Defaults > Sprints and verify duration
input and auto-carryover toggle are visible
- [x] Change duration and carry-over values, save, reload page — verify
persistence
- [x] In a project's Sprint Settings, click "Reset to System Defaults" —
verify it picks up the admin-configured duration and carry-over values
- [ ] Export database, verify new fields appear in the JSON
- [ ] Import a backup without the new fields — verify no errors (fields
are optional in schema)

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

commit.message:
Suppress html2canvas lab() color function error

Added console.error suppression during html2canvas capture to hide
the "Attempting to parse an unsupported color function 'lab'" error.
This is a known limitation of html2canvas with modern CSS colors.

The error is non-blocking and doesn't affect WebGL functionality,
but was cluttering the console. The suppression is temporary and
only active during the snapshot capture.

Created src/main/publish.js with platform API stubs for YouTube, TikTok,
Instagram, and Facebook. Rewired allClips in App.js to derive from
localProjects (rendered clips only), with queue badge count via useMemo.
Replaced videoId checks with renderPath throughout QueueView. Added
"Platform API coming soon" banner. Tracker logging still works for

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix terminated agents flipping to red (waiting_user) on navigation

When a terminated agent's tmux window lingers with empty content,
detect_status returns waiting_user ("Empty pane") instead of terminated.
The revival-detection fix (2c3bed6) re-checks all terminated sessions,
hitting this path and turning them red when focused with j/k.

Now keeps terminated status unless detect_status finds the agent is
actually alive (running, working, etc).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

ci: enable OCR filter to pull Tesseract libs into avfilter link

subtitle_ocr.o is an unconditional libavfilter object that uses
Tesseract via #if CONFIG_LIBTESSERACT. The Tesseract link flags are
only added to EXTRALIBS-avfilter when a filter declares a dependency
on libtesseract. Enable the OCR filter (which has
ocr_filter_deps="libtesseract") to pull in the required libs.

commit.message:
Use source liquid-glass-js library with WebGL controls support

Replaced modified liquid-glass files with source versions from
public/liquid-glass-js/ and added back our WebGL controls integration:

- Copy container.js and button.js from liquid-glass-js source
- Add program reference to gl_refs for both files
- Add continuous render loop with requestAnimationFrame
- Add window.glassControls support for dynamic uniform updates
- Add window exports for Container and Button classes
- Better comments and structure from source library

The source library has improved shape detection (pill, circle, rounded)
and better refraction algorithms while maintaining our controls integration.
